classObjective: Write systems of inequalities that correspond to a given graph and determine whether an ordered pair is a solution to a system without graphing.
workedExamples: 6-5, Mixed Exercises — Write Systems of Inequalities from Graphs; Verify System Solutions by Substitution
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: feasible region interpretation from graphs; substitution checking for system membership; writing systems from bounded regions
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing systems of inequalities from graphs and verifying solutions by substitution
independentPractice: Practice problems writing systems from graphs and determining whether points satisfy systems without graphing
assessment: Exit ticket: write a system of inequalities that represents the feasible region shown in a graph
capReflection: How can you use substitution to check if a point is in the feasible region without graphing?
